body_preview:
Hi Mark,

Sophie officially graduates December 14th and I know we've been dancing around the question, but we need to commit to something. My mom is asking if she should fly in, which means we either need a real plan or I have to tell her it's just us four fo
subject: Hi Mark

body_preview:
Hi Jules,

Tell your mom to book the flight.

I'll put something together in the backyard — not a production, just done well. I'll reach out to Marco this week; he owes me one after I convinced him to take that last-minute Art Museum gig in April. Family-
subject: Re: Hi Jules

body_preview:
Sophie,

You mentioned Mia and Derek's engagement party twice at Easter and then went radio silent. If you're still pulling that together, now is the right time to move — late October in the city means competing with a dozen other events for venue space, e
subject: Sophie

body_preview:
Dad,

I knew the minute I mentioned Mia you were going to go full Campbell Events on me. I'm not complaining.

Here's what I have: roughly 40 people, mix of family and college friends, she wants something that feels like a nice dinner party rather than a
subject: Re: Dad

body_preview:
Sophie,

Marco will do it. 40 people is fine — he actually prefers the smaller ones. Tell him I sent you and give him the February 23rd date before you say anything else. His calendar fills from the middle out.

For the space: look at Fitler Dining Room and
subject: Re: Sophie

body_preview:
Dad,

I've been sitting on this for a few weeks and figured you were the right person to ask — professionally and otherwise. I'm going to propose to Claire. I'm thinking sometime in October, maybe early in the month before things get too hectic with her te
subject: Dad

body_preview:
Ethan,

Good. I was starting to wonder when you were going to stop talking about Claire in the abstract and actually do something.

October works. A few things to nail down first: does she prefer something private or would she be okay with people nearby? T
subject: Re: Ethan

body_preview:
Dad,

She'd want it private, or close to it. She's not someone who needs an audience. Early morning on Kelly Drive actually sounds exactly right — she runs that stretch a few times a week anyway, so it wouldn't feel staged to get her there.

Yes to the ph
subject: Re: Dad

body_preview:
Ethan,

Ring first. Everything else bends around the ring — timing, how you frame it when you ask. Don't plan the morning without it in your pocket.

Photographers: Ryan Kastel (rykastelphoto.com) and Devin Cho. Both have shot proposals, both know how to
subject: Re: Ethan

body_preview:
Becca,

Just got off the phone with Claire Harrington. She wants to swap the cocktail hour passed apps — out with the crab cakes, in with the shrimp skewers. Wedding's December 28th, so we have about ten days before Marco's cutoff.

Can you reach out to him t
subject: Becca

body_preview:
Mark,

Spoke with Marco. He can do the shrimp skewers at the same per-head rate — no upcharge, but he needs written confirmation by Friday EOD to adjust the order. I'll send the swap request over once you give the go-ahead.

Headcount in the Harrington fi
subject: Re: Mark

body_preview:
Linda,

I know you're the last person who should be planning this, but I also know you're the only person who actually knows what Julia would love. I'm putting together a dinner for her 50th — nothing massive, maybe 20 people, somewhere in the city with a
subject: Linda

body_preview:
Linda,

Ha. You're right that I'm the wrong person and also the only person. I'll take that.

For 20, you want a private dining room, not a buyout. Look at Zahav's private room or Vernick's upstairs — both have menus Julia would actually enjoy, and neithe
subject: Re: Linda

body_preview:
Mark,

Vernick is exactly right — I don't know why I didn't think of it first. I'll call them today. And yes, please send me that outside contact for florals; I want something simple and I'd rather not accidentally end up with whoever did the Henderson we
subject: Re: Mark

body_preview:
Marco,

Just got off a call with the Kellerman-Park wedding party — they've updated their dietary breakdown less than five weeks out. Original count had 3 vegan plates for the January 19 reception. New count is 11. That's a significant jump and I want to mak
subject: Marco

body_preview:
Mark,

No problem on the count — 11 vegan plates is completely manageable. The mushroom Wellington holds well at volume and I'm comfortable with it. Where it gets interesting is the cauliflower purée we'd been pairing with it; I'd want to swap in roasted
subject: Re: Mark

body_preview:
Marco,

Fingerlings over the purée — agreed, that's the right call. I'll let the couple know the adjustment; they won't care as long as the plate looks intentional, which it does.

I can't make the 24th — I have the Nguyen-Walsh rehearsal dinner that afte
subject: Re: Marco

body_preview:
Mark,

Becca on the 24th is perfect — I'll connect with her directly to confirm a time. She's been solid to work with on logistics and it'll be faster than going back and forth through email anyway.

Revised quote attached. The vegan plate adjustment adde
subject: Re: Mark
